title: cascabela thevetioides
intro: Cascabela thevetioides, also known as the Calabash tree or the Thevetia tree, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its heart-shaped seeds, which contain a toxic alkaloid called thevetin. The seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Cascabela thevetioides is often prepared as a herbal tea or used in tinctures to leverage its medicinal benefits, although it is essential to note that the seeds are toxic and should be handled with caution.
